paper_title: Field Sampling and Analysis of Rawapening Lake

methods.method_name: Field Sampling and Water Quality Analysis
methods.description: This method involves the collection of diatom and water quality samples from various substrates across Rawapening Lake, along with a bathymetric study to guide coring activities. The purpose is to analyze water quality variables and sediment composition to understand the lake's ecological history.

steps.description: In 2008, an intensive regime of diatom and water quality sampling was undertaken covering a range of substrates across the Lake.
steps.searchable_summary: collect diatom and water quality samples from lake substrates

steps.description: The water quality variables analyzed comprising dissolved oxygen, temperature, conductivity, turbidity and pH.
A comprehensive bathymetric study was undertaken on Rawapening Lake in August 2008.
Echosounding of the lake reveals extensive areas of less than 2 m depth but three deeper depocentres in the lake's west of up to 18 m depth. This was used to direct coring activities.
steps.searchable_summary: use echosounding data to guide coring activities

steps.description: At multiple core sites surface sediment samples and water samples were taken from which total nitrogen, total phosphorous, silica, lead, cadmium, chromium and copper were analyzed.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and analyze sediment and water samples for chemical composition

methods.description: This method involves the preparation and analysis of sediment subsamples for diatom identification and the reconstruction of nutrient histories using software tools.

steps.description: The collected sediment cores ranged in length from 29-63 cm. These were sub sampled in fine resolution (0.5 cm) for diatoms.
steps.searchable_summary: subsample sediment cores for diatom analysis

steps.description: Sediment subsamples were prepared following Last and Smol (2001), identified following standard texts (Krammer and Lange-Bertalot, 2010) and by comparison with the reference collections held at Diponegoro University and The University of Ballarat.
Counts of a minimum of 300 valves were taken on a subset of samples.
The software ERNIE (Juggins, 2001) was used to reconstruct nutrient histories of the lake.
steps.searchable_summary: use ERNIE software to reconstruct nutrient histories

steps.description: The fossil diatom assemblages are presented in the data management and drawing package C2 1.5.1 (Juggins, 2003).
steps.searchable_summary: visualize diatom assemblages using C2 software
